options.description: show help for mgnl

subcommands.name: add-availability
subcommands.description: this command makes a component available to a page

options.description: path to the light module that contains the page template
add this parameter to autogenerate the component instead of providing plain availability

states.name: <[module-id:]path-to-component>
states.description: the component you want to make available

states.name: <path-to-page[@area]>
states.description: the page you want to make the component available to

subcommands.name: create-app
subcommands.description: this command creates a content type and an app

states.name: name
states.description: the name of the new app. The name cannot contain spaces

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module where the app is created

states.name: blockName
states.description: the name of the new block definition. The block name cannot contain spaces

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module to add the block to

subcommands.name: create-component
subcommands.description: this command creates a new component template

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module for the component template
the page you want to make the component available to
add this parameter to autogenerate the component instead of providing plain availability

states.name: name
states.description: the name of the new component template. The name cannot contain spaces

subcommands.name: create-content-type
subcommands.description: this command creates a content type

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module where the content type is created
creates also a new app that references the content type. With the -a option, the command is functionally equivalent to the create-app command

states.name: name
states.description: the name of the new content type. The name cannot contain spaces

subcommands.name: create-light-module
subcommands.description: this command creates a new light module in the form of a set of empty light module folders

states.name: moduleName
states.description: the name of the new light module. Avoid spaces and special characters since this name is used as folder name

options.description: prototype variant to use
parent directory for the new light module

subcommands.name: create-page
subcommands.description: this command creates a new page template

states.name: templateName
states.description: the name of the new page template. The template name cannot contain spaces

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module to add the page template to

subcommands.name: create-virtual-uri
subcommands.description: the command creates a virtual URI mapping configuration file (a YAML definition file) in the light module

options.description: prototype variant to use
path to the light module
the pattern to be matched in the requested URI. Enclose the <uri> value in quotes
a concrete URI that the request is mapped to. Enclose the <prefix:uri> value in quotes

states.name: virtualUriName
states.description: the name of the new URI configuration

subcommands.name: customize-local-config
subcommands.description: run this command to create a local configuration

options.description: path to install mgnl-cli-prototypes and mgnl-cli.json

subcommands.name: install
subcommands.description: downloads and installs one or more light modules from npm to the light module directory

states.name: light-module-name
states.description: at least one name of a light module to be downloaded from npm. If you are installing more than one module from the repository, separate the module names with a

options.description: path to the light-modules folder

subcommands.name: jumpstart
subcommands.description: this command downloads, unpacks and pre-configures a Magnolia Tomcat server together with a specific webapp

options.description: path to the light modules root folder
use to choose a specific version of the Magnolia webapp. If not provided, the latest stable version of the specified webapp is downloaded
if provided, a sample light module under the light modules root folder with the given name is created
downloads the latest snapshot development version of the specified webapp
use to directly specify which Magnolia webapp you want to install

subcommands.name: tab-completion
subcommands.description: run the command to install or uninstall autocompletion for Magnolia CLI commands

subcommands.name: search
subcommands.description: searches for Magnolia-related packages available from npm

states.name: query
states.description: the search query sent to the npm's API. The query can contain multiple terms separated with commas and no spaces

subcommands.name: start
subcommands.description: this command starts up Magnolia and displays the main log file

options.description: path to the apache-tomcat folder
does NOT ignore the open files limit check. The files limit check is ignored by default
